title: Select a team from a person attribute in a form

posts.author: thomasgaillard (Thomas Gaillard)
posts.cookedHtml:
In a form, it is currently impossible to assign a team to a person field.
This is possible from a table, but not from a form.
A workaround is possible via automation, but this involves creating duplicate attributes and it would be great to be able to do this directly from a form.

Thanks a lot!

posts.author: Schteevynn (Stephen)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree, this needs to be implemented. Users should be able to choose a team from a form, especially if they are a member of that team (e.g. an individual submitting a request on behalf of their whole team).

The value can be changed to a team on the board after the fact, but why not allow it on the form?

posts.author: yossir.rousso (Yossi)
posts.cookedHtml: I believe it’s time to revive this topic, as it’s been a while. Is there a prospect for this in the roadmap?

posts.author: Nachliel (Nachliel from Easyapps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @yossir.rousso @thomasgaillard @Schteevynn

Our monday app EasyForm supports selecting teams for the People column.

In addition to that, with EasyFrom you can select what users and teams will be displayed on the form. Please checkout our documentation site for more details.

If you want help setting this up or have any questions, you can reach us at easyformsupport@easyapphq.com or book a call with us here.
